source_pr.title: tools: add a script to support patch go
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #41575, ref #41574

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

patch-go.sh will find the go source code, apply the patch to it according to the go version.
The patch is indeed this PR https://github.com/golang/go/pull/51347

Patch go:

```
$ ./tools/patch-go/patch-go.sh
patching go1.20.5
locate go src at /home/genius/project/go
apply patch  /home/genius/project/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/tools/patch-go/go1.20.5.patch
```

If go is patched already, the script display this:

```
$ ./tools/patch-go/patch-go.sh
go is patched already
if you want to reverse the patch, use ./patch-go.sh -R
```

And if you want to revert the patch:

```
$ ./tools/patch-go/patch-go.sh -R
patching go1.20.5
locate go src at /home/genius/project/go
apply patch -R /home/genius/project/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/tools/patch-go/go1.20.5.patch
```

If the patch does not exist for that go version:

```
$ ./patch-go.sh
patch file not exist for version go1.20.5
```

After using the patched go, it's possible to build https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/41565

Documentation

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

